steps.title: Spot the 'resemblance' signal
steps.content: The phrase “would see the resemblance” is the decisive clue. It signals that the blank needs a noun for something that reproduces or copies Faulkner's style — something visibly similar to his work because it replicates it.

steps.title: Match to the correct noun
steps.content:
We need a noun meaning a copy or reproduction of Faulkner's style. “Imitation” directly fits this meaning—it names what Forrest does by writing a passage that replicates Faulkner's style.

The other choices are clearly wrong: forgetting and rejection contradict the admiring, tributive context, and opinion doesn't fit the noun slot describing what the passage IS.

That matches Choice C — imitation.
